title: A letter to the night cleaner
summary: An office worker writes a short letter to the cleaner he has never met, who works after he goes home.
body:
Dear Arthur,

We have never met. You clean my office at night. I leave at six. You come at nine. I do not know your face. But I know you in another way.

Every morning, my desk is clean. The bin is empty. My plant has water. I did not ask for that. The plant is in the corner. It is small. Most people do not see it. But you see it. You give it water.

Last week I was very tired. I worked late. I left a note on my desk by mistake. It said, 'I cannot do this job. I am not good enough.' I forgot the note there all night.

In the morning, the note was gone. Next to my keyboard, there was a new note. It said, 'Your plant grew two new leaves this week. Small things grow slowly. You are doing fine. — A.'

I sat down. I did not work for ten minutes. I just looked at the plant.

We still have never met. Maybe we never will. You work in the dark hours. I work in the light ones. But I want to say thank you. Not for the clean desk. For the leaves. For seeing the small thing that I could not see myself.

Yours,
Daniel

annotations.meaningVi: Kính gửi / Thân gửi (mở đầu thư)

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect with 'never'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại hoàn thành với 'never' để nói một việc CHƯA TỪNG xảy ra cho đến giờ. Trong bài: 'We have never met.' (Chúng ta chưa từng gặp nhau.) 'Never' đứng giữa 'have' và động từ phân từ (met). Đây là dạng phủ định, nên không thêm 'not'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present perfect with 'never' to say something has NOT happened up to now. From the letter: 'We have never met.' 'Never' goes between 'have' and the past participle (met). It already makes the sentence negative, so you do not add 'not'.

translationVi:
Kính gửi Arthur,

Chúng ta chưa từng gặp nhau. Anh dọn văn phòng của tôi vào ban đêm. Tôi về lúc sáu giờ. Anh đến lúc chín giờ. Tôi không biết mặt anh. Nhưng tôi biết anh theo một cách khác.

Mỗi sáng, bàn tôi sạch sẽ. Thùng rác trống. Cây của tôi có nước. Tôi không hề nhờ làm vậy. Cây ở trong góc. Nó nhỏ. Hầu hết mọi người không để ý đến nó. Nhưng anh thấy nó. Anh tưới nước cho nó.

Tuần trước tôi rất mệt. Tôi làm muộn. Tôi vô tình để quên một mảnh giấy trên bàn. Nó viết: 'Tôi không làm nổi công việc này. Tôi không đủ giỏi.' Tôi để quên mảnh giấy đó suốt đêm.

Sáng ra, mảnh giấy biến mất. Cạnh bàn phím của tôi có một mảnh giấy mới. Nó viết: 'Cây của anh mọc thêm hai lá mới tuần này. Những điều nhỏ lớn lên từ từ. Anh đang ổn mà. — A.'

Tôi ngồi xuống. Tôi không làm việc trong mười phút. Tôi chỉ nhìn cái cây.

Chúng ta vẫn chưa từng gặp nhau. Có lẽ sẽ chẳng bao giờ. Anh làm ca tối. Tôi làm ca ngày. Nhưng tôi muốn nói cảm ơn. Không phải vì cái bàn sạch. Vì những chiếc lá. Vì đã thấy điều nhỏ bé mà chính tôi không thấy được.

Thân ái,
Daniel

questions.prompt: Who is the letter written to?
questions.explanation: 'Dear Arthur... You clean my office at night.'

questions.prompt: Why have the two men never met?
options: Daniel leaves at six and Arthur comes at nine
Daniel never goes to the office
questions.explanation: 'I leave at six. You come at nine.'

questions.prompt: What small thing does Arthur do for the plant?
questions.explanation: 'My plant has water... You give it water.'

questions.prompt: What did Daniel's first note say?
options: 'I cannot do this job. I am not good enough.'
questions.explanation: Direct quote from the note he forgot.

questions.prompt: What did the new note next to the keyboard say about the plant?
options: It grew two new leaves that week
questions.explanation: 'Your plant grew two new leaves this week.'

questions.prompt: What did Daniel do after reading the note?
options: He sat and just looked at the plant for ten minutes

questions.prompt: What is Daniel really thanking Arthur for?
options: For seeing the small thing Daniel could not see himself
questions.explanation: 'Not for the clean desk. For the leaves. For seeing the small thing that I could not see myself.'

questions.prompt: What does 'the dark hours' and 'the light ones' mean?
options: Arthur works at night and Daniel works in the day
One man is sad and one is happy
questions.explanation: 'You work in the dark hours. I work in the light ones.'
questions.hint: Vocabulary in context, near the end.

questions.prompt: Why is Arthur's message comforting to Daniel?
options: It quietly answers Daniel's fear that he is not good enough
questions.explanation: 'Small things grow slowly. You are doing fine.' answers the lost note.

options: A small, unseen act of kindness can reach someone deeply.
Plants need a lot of water.
Working late is good for you.
questions.explanation: Arthur's tiny care with the plant and note moved Daniel.

cloze.template:
Dear Arthur,

We have never {{b1}}. You clean my office at night. I leave at six. You come at nine. I do not know your face. But I know you in another way.

Every morning, my {{b2}} is clean. The bin is empty. My plant has {{b3}}. I did not ask for that. The plant is in the {{b4}}. It is small. Most people do not see it. But you see it. You give it water.

Last week I was very tired. I worked late. I left a note on my desk by {{b5}}. It said, 'I cannot do this job. I am not good {{b6}}.' I forgot the note there all night.

In the morning, the note was gone. Next to my keyboard, there was a new note. It said, 'Your plant grew two new {{b7}} this week. Small things grow slowly. You are doing {{b8}}. — A.'

I sat down. I did not work for ten minutes. I just looked at the plant.

We still have never met. Maybe we never will. You work in the {{b9}} hours. I work in the light ones. But I want to say thank you. Not for the clean desk. For the leaves. For seeing the small thing that I could not see {{b10}}.

Yours,
Daniel

blanks.explanation: The plant is in the corner.
That I could not see myself.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Arthur noticed two new leaves and used them to answer a fear Daniel never spoke aloud. Has anyone ever shown they understood something about you that you had not said out loud — and how did a small sign make that clear?
